name: Wirken: per-agent LLM cost anomaly
message:
A Wirken agent's LLM spend in the last hour exceeded 3x its own trailing 7-day mean hourly spend.

Detection: agent_cost_anomaly (9)
Severity baseline: medium

The numerator is the agent's spend in the most recent 1-hour bucket; the denominator is the trailing 7-day moving average of that agent's hourly spend, which is the agent's own baseline. The ratio breaching 3 means a step change in this agent's spend rate, not an absolute dollar threshold.

Tunables and defaults: the `.rollup(sum, 3600)` bucket is the window (1h, catches a step change within an hour); the `moving_rollup(..., 604800, 'avg')` lookback is the baseline period (7d, smooths daily and weekly cycles); the `> 3` is the multiplier (3x normal spend is a clear step). Raise the multiplier to reduce sensitivity; widen the window to smooth bursty agents.

This monitor reads the log-based metric `wirken.llm.cost_usd_micros` (measure `@wirken.event.total_cost_usd_micros`, tagged by agent_id); see datadog/README.md for the metric definition. Requires audit schema 1.6.0+ cost fields and the LlmResponse forwarder opt-in.

Agents with under a day of history read a thin baseline; treat their first alerts as advisory.

@security-team
